title: MATLAB files to calculate crease breadth values on 3D preserved impressions.
author: Dzemila Sero
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The folder "CODE" contains MATLAB files to reproduce the methods for crease breadth calculation in 3D, as described in "Artist profiling using micro-CT scanning of a Rijksmuseum terracotta sculpture" by Sero et al. 

 In "main.m", we show the step-by-step procedure to calculate crease breadth values. This file calls functions that read an *.obj file containing the 3D model (previously brushed in MeshLab), enhance the 3D partial impression, find the intersects between a straight line and a 3D mesh, and visualize the results. 

 We developed our codes in MATLAB R2022a. It is necessary to install the "Curve Fitting Toolbox" in MATLAB to run surface fitting. 

 "Data.obj" is the 3D model of a partial impression left on fresh clay, which is not fired. The piece of clay was CT-scanned and the mesh is generated following the procedure described in our manuscript. 

 The folder "toolbox" contains MATLAB functions used in "main.m". In addition, we include "RunKAmod.m" and "RunKAmod2.m" for age estimation. 

 The folder "example figures" contains figures of the steps implemented in "main.m". It also includes "Data_Round1.mat", which is a *.mat file with the results obtained by running "main.m" on "Data.obj". 

 All files are written by Dzemila Sero, D.Sero@rijksmuseum.nl, copyright 2023.
